person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography
Research Notes«/b»
From her husband's profile (Roger Lewknor, copied over before editing his profile on 19 December 2019):

Her parentage must be considered uncertain at this point. Richardson records that she was the daughter of Nicholas Carew Esq of Beddington and Warlingham, Surrey, and his 1st wife Isabel, daughter of Alice de la Mare), but qualifies it by saying she was "evidently" so.

Many other genealogies assert that her parents were John Carew, including a relatively credible site called "Tudor Place."

To add to the confusion, a collection of historical Staffordshire stories reports that Roger Lewknor, who resided at Bradhurst, Co. Sussex, married Margaret, daughter of Sir John Carew, of Molesford, Co. Bucks, Knight, and died 1401.

«b»Some genealogies also erroneously show:«/b»
* Roger married instead to Margaret, daughter of John Carew of Molesford.
* Thomas married to Elizabeth daughter of Nicholas Carew of Beddington - she was in fact his mother.
* Thomas married to Catherine - she was in fact his daughter-in-law.

«b»Note other possible parentage of Elizabeth Carew«/b»
The International Society of Royal Descendants (ISRD)

Sir Roger Lewkenor - b: abt 1369 in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, d: abt 1391 in Mallerforde, Buckinghamshire & Elizabeth Carew - b: abt 1372 in Mallerforde, Buckinghamshire - d: 1410 daughter of: Nicholas Carew - b: 1362 d: 1432 & Isabel Delamare they had four children - including an Elizabeth (above) He was also married to: Mercy Hayme and they had 14 children - She was daughter of: Stephen Hayme & Christine.

son of: Nicholas Carew - d: 1390 & Lucy Willoughby

son of: Thomas Carew of Moulsford - b: abt 1292 d: bef 1337 & Maud Pauncefote - m: bet 1323-1328 - Her first marriage: John le Strange

son of: Nicholas Carew of Moulsford - b: abt 1265 d: bef 1311 & Avice Martin - b: abt 1265

son of: Nicholas Carew - Baron Carew & Avice Tuite - daughter of: Richard Tuite - Lord of Master

«b»Birth«/b»
Elizabeth Carew was born about 1372 in Mallerford, Buckinghamshire.

«b»Parentage«/b»
Elizabeth's parentage is uncertain and awaiting solid docmentation.

Most internet sites including "Tudor Place" show Elizabeth's father as John Carew, who died 1346/7 in the Seige of Calais. He in turn was the son of John de Carew, Baron Carew, and Margaret Mohun. Elizabeth's mother was Agnes Martin. Elizabeth was the only child.

Richardson, on the other hand, asserts tentatively that Elizabeth Carew, Roger Lewknor's wife, was "evidently" daughter of Nicholas Carew by his first wife Isabel, daughter of Alice de la Mare, of Delamare (in Wheathampstead) Hertfordshire.

Nicholas Carew was:
of Beddington and Warlingham, Surrey
Knight of the Shire for Surrey
Sheriff of Surrey and Sussex 

«b»1st Marriage to Roger Lewknor, and Children of that Marriage«/b»
About 1391 in Mallerforde, Buckinghamshire, she married Roger Lewknor (b. ABT 1369 - d. BEF 1405) It is certain that they had three children and may have had more:

1.) Thomas, Knt., b. abt 1392, d. 22 Jun 1452. Shown as born in Horsted Keynes.

2.) John, Knt, born abt 1395

3.) Agnes (or Anne) wife of Andrew Sackville, Richard Mhyll, Esq, and William Kighley, b. abt 1393. Shown as born at Broadhurst, Horsted Keynes

4.) Eleanor Lewknor, not named by Richardson or Tudor Place, but named by Staffordshire. She is shown as born 1394 in Ranton, Staffordshire, which is the estate of her husband, not hers.

5.) Walter Lewknor. Shown as born in 1396 in Goring, Sussex, which is the estate of a different Lewknor family.

6.) Margaret Lewknor. Shown as born in 1399 in Broadhurst, Horsted Keynes.

2nd Marriage to Robert Knyvet
Elizabeth married 2nd, as his 2nd wife, Robert Knyvet, Esq., younger son of John Knyvet, Knt., by Eleanor, daughter of Ralph Basset, Knt.

«b»1410 Death«/b»
Elizabeth Carew died 25 Sep 1410 and was buried at Beddington, Surrey.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Carew於1372年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，她的父親John de Carew26歲，母親Agnes Martin27歲。她和Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex至少有1個兒子。她去世於1410年9月25日在英國素可霍斯特德 凱恩斯享年38歲，埋葬在英國素可霍斯特德 凱恩斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Carew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, James Patrick, Kieran.

Welsh (Pembrokeshire): habitational name from any of various minor places in Wales, in particular one near Pembroke with a major castle, named from Welsh caer ‘fort’ + rhiw ‘hill, slope’. The surname was taken to Ireland soon after the Norman invasion of England and was Gaelicized as de Carrún. In the 16th century a family called Carew from Devon played an important part in establishing the Tudor administration in Ireland.
Cornish (Somerset and Devon): from the cognate Cornish word kerrow (plural of ker ‘fort’), which occurs seven times as a placename in Cornwall.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Carew was born in 1372, in Buckinghamshire, England, her father, John de Carew, was 26 and her mother, Agnes Martin, was 27. She had at least 1 son with Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. She died on 25 September 1410, in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Carew est née en 1372, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Carew, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Agnes Martin, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. Elle est décédée le 25 septembre 1410, à Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Carew 1372 in Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Carew 26 und ihre Mutter Agnes Martin 27. Sie hatte mit Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 25. September 1410 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England und wurde in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Carew è nata il nel 1372 nel Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, John de Carew, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Agnes Martin, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. Morì il 25 settembre 1410, nel Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Carewが1372年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父John de Carewは26歳，母Agnes Martinは27歳であった。彼女はSir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussexとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1410年9月25日にイギリススセックスホールステド ケインスで38歳で死去し，イギリススセックスホールステド ケインスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Carew(이)가 1372년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Carew(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Martin(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 9월 25일 영국 수세스 호르스테드 케인스에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 호르스테드 케인스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Carew nasceu em 1372, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Carew, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Martin, tinha 27 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. Ela faleceu em 25 de setembro de 1410, em Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Carew родилась в 1372 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Carew было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Martin, 27. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. Она умерла 25 сентября 1410 г. в местности хаурстед кинесь, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности хаурстед кинесь, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Carew nació en 1372, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Carew, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Agnes Martin, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Roger Lewknor of Horsted Keynes Sussex. Murió el 25 de septiembre de 1410, en Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Horsted Keynes, Sussex, Inglaterra.
